questions.question: What should NOT be committed to Git in an ML project:
options: Large binary files: model weights (.pkl, .pt, .h5), datasets (.csv, .parquet), generated outputs
questions.explanation: Large/binary files bloat repos and can't be diffed. Use S3/HuggingFace Hub/DVC instead.

questions.question: DVC (Data Version Control) is used for:
options: Versioning large data files and model artifacts alongside code without storing them in Git
questions.explanation: dvc add data/train.csv creates a small .dvc pointer file committed to Git. Data goes to S3.

questions.question: A Git branch for an experiment should be:
questions.explanation: Descriptive branch names make it clear what each branch is testing without reading the code.

questions.question: git stash is used to:
options: Temporarily save uncommitted changes so you can switch branches or pull without losing work
questions.explanation: git stash; git checkout other-branch; git stash pop -- safely handles context switches.

questions.question: A .gitignore for ML should exclude:
questions.explanation: Exclude Python cache, virtual envs, data, model artifacts, secrets, and ML tool outputs.

questions.question: git blame is used to:
options: Show which commit and author last modified each line of a file
questions.explanation: Useful for understanding when and why code changed. Not an accusation tool.

questions.question: Semantic versioning (semver) for ML models uses:
options: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH: MAJOR=breaking changes, MINOR=new features, PATCH=bug fixes
questions.explanation: model-v2.1.3: v2=breaking API change, v2.1=new feature, v2.1.3=bug fix.

questions.question: Git tags are used in ML to:
options: Mark specific commits as release points (e.g. model-v1.0, paper-submission)
questions.explanation: git tag -a v1.0 -m 'First production model' marks important milestones permanently.

questions.question: Pull Requests (PRs) in ML workflows:
options: Enforce code review before merging experiment code to main -- critical for team ML projects
questions.explanation: PRs with automated CI checks (data validation, model tests) prevent bad code/models reaching production.

questions.question: GitHub Actions for ML CI/CD automates:
options: Data validation, model training, performance testing, and deployment on every push
questions.explanation: on: push triggers the pipeline. if: success() gates deployment on all checks passing.
